39	// ClassifyStop maps scattered drain-loop terminal checks to a single typed

41	func ClassifyStop(in StopInput) (StopDecision, bool) {
42		switch {
43		case in.ContextErr != nil:
44			return StopDecision{
45				Condition:  StopConditionSignal,
46				ExitReason: signalExitReason(in.ContextErr),
47			}, true
48		case in.Budget:
49			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionBudget, ExitReason: "budget"}, true
50		case in.NoProgress:
51			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionNoProgress, ExitReason: "no_progress"}, true
52		case in.Blocked:
53			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionBlocked, ExitReason: "blocked"}, true
54		case in.Once:
55			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionOnce, ExitReason: "once_complete"}, true
56		case in.NoReadyWork && in.PollInterval <= 0:
57			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionDrained, ExitReason: "explicit_poll_zero"}, true
58		default:
59			return StopDecision{}, false
60		}
61	}

67		case context.DeadlineExceeded:
68			return "sigterm"
69		default:
70			return "context_cancelled"
71		}

52		case in.Blocked:
53			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionBlocked, ExitReason: "blocked"}, true
54		case in.Once:
55			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionOnce, ExitReason: "once_complete"}, true
56		case in.NoReadyWork && in.PollInterval <= 0:
57			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionDrained, ExitReason: "explicit_poll_zero"}, true
58		default:
59			return StopDecision{}, false
60		}
61	}
62	
63	func signalExitReason(err error) string {
64		switch err {
65		case context.Canceled:
66			return "sigint"
67		case context.DeadlineExceeded:
68			return "sigterm"
69		default:
70			return "context_cancelled"
71		}
72	}

114	func runStopConditionCase(t *testing.T, worker *ExecuteBeadWorker, ctx context.Context, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, string, error) {

117		var sink bytes.Buffer

120		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
121		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
122		result, err := worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, runtime)
123		exitReason := loopEndExitReason(t, sink.String())
124		return result, exitReason, err
125	}

127	func loopEndExitReason(t *testing.T, raw string) string {
128		t.Helper()
129		for _, line := range strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(raw), "\n") {
130			if strings.TrimSpace(line) == "" {
131				continue
132			}
133			var entry map[string]any
134			require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &entry))
135			if entry["type"] != "loop.end" {
136				continue
137			}
138			data, ok := entry["data"].(map[string]any)
139			require.True(t, ok, "loop.end data missing")
140			reason, _ := data["exit_reason"].(string)
141			return reason
142		}
143		require.Fail(t, "loop.end event not found", raw)
144		return ""
145	}

22	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
23	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
24	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
25	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
26	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
27		Log             io.Writer

29		CleanupRunner   executionCleanupRunner
30		CleanupInterval time.Duration
31		CleanupTickCh   <-chan time.Time
32		EventSink       io.Writer
33		ProgressCh      chan<- ProgressEvent
34		PreClaimHook    func(ctx context.Context) error
35		// PreClaimIntakeHook runs after routing preflight and before Claim. It
36		// classifies the candidate for actionability/scope; only
37		// actionable_atomic proceeds directly to Claim. Non-atomic outcomes
38		// skip the bead without claim. intake_error fail-opens so infrastructure
39		// failures do not become hidden dispatch failures.
40		PreClaimIntakeHook    PreClaimIntakeHook
41		PreDispatchLintHook   func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (LintResult, error)
42		PostAttemptTriageHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error)
43		// ReviewCostCap, when non-nil, accumulates reviewer cost on the same
44		// loop budget tracker used by the implementer attempts.
45		ReviewCostCap *escalation.CostCapTracker
46		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, runs once during Run bootstrap before
47		// the drain loop starts. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute
48		// against the loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed
49		// routing error the upstream surfaced — notably
50		// agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible when the harness allow-list rejects
51		// the model. Any non-nil error stops the worker before any bead is
52		// claimed; DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list.
53		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
54		// BudgetStop, when non-nil, is checked before selecting the next bead. It
55		// lets CLI/server callers surface already-tripped budget state as a typed
56		// work-layer StopCondition before any new bead is claimed.
57		BudgetStop   func() (ExecuteBeadReport, bool)

66		// named bead from the execution-ready queue. Used by `ddx try <bead-id>`

2	
3	import "strings"
4	
5	// OperatorCancelReason marks a landing preserved because the operator
6	// cancelled the attempt mid-flight via /api/beads/<id>/cancel. ADR-022
7	// §Cancel SLA: the worker aborts at the next safe point (between LLM turns /
8	// git ops) and emits a preserved_for_review result with this exact reason.
9	const OperatorCancelReason = "operator_cancel"
10	
11	// PushFailedReasonPrefix is the canonical Reason prefix written by the
12	// landing layer when `git push` rejects the new tip after a local merge or
13	// fast-forward succeeded. ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus matches on this prefix
14	// to map a merged-locally-but-not-pushed outcome to push_failed instead of
15	// success, so the bead stays open.
16	const PushFailedReasonPrefix = "landed locally; push failed:"
17	
18	// PushConflictReasonPrefix marks a more specific failure mode than
19	// PushFailedReasonPrefix: the local push was rejected because the remote
20	// advanced, the loop attempted an automatic pull/merge to recover, and the
21	// merge produced a conflict the loop cannot resolve. The bead is parked for
22	// human review with a short cooldown rather than a year-long park.
23	const PushConflictReasonPrefix = "landed locally; push conflict:"
24	
25	// FailureMode constants classify *why* an execution did not land cleanly.
26	// They are orthogonal to Outcome/Status: a single failed attempt carries one
27	// outcome (e.g. preserved) and one failure mode (e.g. merge_conflict) so
28	// measurement surfaces can aggregate failures by cause.
29	const (
30		FailureModeContextOverflow                = "context_overflow"
31		FailureModeMergeConflict                  = "merge_conflict"
32		FailureModeTestFailure                    = "test_failure"
33		FailureModeBuildFailure                   = "build_failure"
34		FailureModeTimeout                        = "timeout"
35		FailureModeAuthError                      = "auth_error"
36		FailureModeNoChanges                      = "no_changes"
37		FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced             = "no_evidence_produced"
38		FailureModeRatchetMiss                    = "ratchet_miss"
39		FailureModeNoViableProvider               = "no_viable_provider"
40		FailureModeHarnessNotInstalled            = "harness_not_installed"
41		FailureModeBlockedByPassthroughConstraint = "blocked_by_passthrough_constraint"
42		FailureModeAgentPowerUnsatisfied          = "agent_power_unsatisfied"
43		FailureModeLockContention                 = "lock_contention"
44		FailureModeUnknown                        = "unknown"
45	)
46	
47	// ResourceExhaustedStopMessage is the operator-visible message emitted when
48	// execution must stop because the host cannot safely continue draining the
49	// queue.
50	const ResourceExhaustedStopMessage = "resource exhausted after cleanup; stopping work loop"
51	
52	// ClassifyFailureMode derives a failure_mode for a worker-level result from
53	// its outcome, exit code, and error message. Returns "" when the outcome is
54	// task_succeeded (success carries no failure mode). task_no_changes always
55	// maps to FailureModeNoChanges regardless of error text.
56	//
57	// For task_failed (or non-zero exit code), the error message is scanned for
58	// well-known substrings. The order matters: context/auth/timeout patterns
59	// are checked before generic test/build patterns so a "test timed out"
60	// classifies as timeout rather than test_failure. Anything unrecognised
61	// maps to FailureModeUnknown so aggregates never hide a missed pattern.
62	func ClassifyFailureMode(outcome string, exitCode int, errMsg string) string {
63		switch outcome {
64		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
65			if exitCode == 0 {
66				return ""
67			}
68			// Degenerate: success outcome with non-zero exit. Treat as unknown
69			// failure rather than success so the aggregate flags the anomaly.
70		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
71			return FailureModeNoChanges
72		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
73			return FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced
74		}
75	
76		lower := strings.ToLower(errMsg)
77		switch {
78		case IsLockContentionError(errMsg):
79			return FailureModeLockContention
80		case containsAny(lower,
81			"passthrough constraint unsatisfiable",
82			"passthrough constraint:",
83			"max_power is less than min_power",
84			"max_power exceeds min_power",
85			"no viable routing candidate for pins",
86			"harness cannot satisfy power constraint",
87			"harness pin incompatible with power bounds",
88			"model pin incompatible with power bounds",
89			"provider pin incompatible with power bounds"):
90			return FailureModeBlockedByPassthroughConstraint
91		case containsAny(lower,
92			"agent power unsatisfied",
93			"no model meets min_power",
94			"no model with power >=",
95			"minimum power not achievable",
96			"min_power constraint cannot be satisfied"):
97			return FailureModeAgentPowerUnsatisfied
98		case containsAny(lower,
99			"executable file not found in $path",
100			"executable file not found",

100			"executable file not found",
101			"no such file or directory") &&
102			containsAny(lower, "exec:", "harness", "claude", "agent", "codex", "gemini"):
103			return FailureModeHarnessNotInstalled
104		case containsAny(lower,
105			"no viable harness",
106			"no harness configured",
107			"failed to initialize routing service",
108			"no viable provider"):
109			return FailureModeNoViableProvider
110		case containsAny(lower,
111			"context length",
112			"context_length_exceeded",
113			"context window",
114			"maximum context",
115			"prompt is too long",
116			"prompt too long",
117			"token limit",
118			"too many tokens",
119			"exceeds the context"):
120			return FailureModeContextOverflow
121		case containsAny(lower,
122			"unauthorized",
123			"401 unauthorized",
124			"invalid api key",
125			"invalid_api_key",
126			"authentication failed",
127			"authentication_error",
128			"no api key",
129			"missing api key",
130			"quota exceeded",
131			"insufficient quota",
132			"insufficient_quota",
133			"rate limit",
134			"rate_limit",
135			"429"):
136			return FailureModeAuthError
137		case containsAny(lower,
138			"timeout",
139			"timed out",
140			"context deadline exceeded",
141			"deadline exceeded",
142			"signal: killed"):
143			return FailureModeTimeout
144		case containsAny(lower,
145			"merge conflict",
146			"ff-merge not possible",
147			"merge failed",
148			"automatic merge failed"):
149			return FailureModeMergeConflict
150		case containsAny(lower,
151			"build failed",
152			"compilation failed",
153			"compile error",
154			"compilation error",
155			"cannot find package",
156			"undefined:",
157			"undefined reference",
158			"syntax error"):
159			return FailureModeBuildFailure
160		case containsAny(lower,
161			"test failed",
162			"tests failed",
163			"--- fail:",
164			"assertion failed",
165			"test failure"):
166			return FailureModeTestFailure
167		}
168		if exitCode != 0 {
169			return FailureModeUnknown
170		}
171		return ""
172	}
173	
174	// containsAny reports whether s contains any of the given substrings. s is
175	// assumed to already be lower-cased by the caller.
176	func containsAny(s string, needles ...string) bool {
177		for _, n := range needles {
178			if n == "" {
179				continue
180			}
181			if strings.Contains(s, n) {
182				return true
183			}
184		}
185		return false
186	}
187	
188	// classifyLandingFailureMode computes the final failure_mode for a result
189	// after the orchestrator has made its landing decision. It prefers
190	// landing-specific signals (merge conflict, gate failure) over the worker's
191	// classification and falls back to workerMode for worker-level failures
192	// (agent execution failed, context overflow, etc.). Returns "" when the
193	// bead merged cleanly.
194	func classifyLandingFailureMode(landingOutcome, landingReason string, gateResults []GateCheckResult, workerMode string) string {
195		switch landingOutcome {
196		case "merged":
197			return ""
198		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
199			return FailureModeNoChanges
200		case "no-evidence", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
201			return FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced
202		case "error":
203			if workerMode != "" {
204				return workerMode
205			}
206			return FailureModeUnknown
207		case "preserved":
208			switch landingReason {
209			case "merge conflict", "merge failed", "ff-merge not possible":
210				return FailureModeMergeConflict
211			case "post-run checks failed":
212				return classifyGateFailure(gateResults)
213			case RatchetPreserveReason:
214				return FailureModeRatchetMiss
215			case OperatorCancelReason:
216				return ""
217			}
218			// Other preserved reasons (e.g. "agent execution failed",
219			// "--no-merge specified") defer to the worker's classification.
220			if workerMode != "" {
221				return workerMode
222			}
223			return FailureModeUnknown
224		}
225		if workerMode != "" {
226			return workerMode
227		}
228		return FailureModeUnknown
229	}
230	
231	// classifyGateFailure inspects failed gate results to distinguish
232	// build_failure from test_failure. Gate definitions that fail to compile
233	// before running (go build, tsc, cargo build) typically surface build
234	// diagnostics in stderr; test runners surface test failure markers. When
235	// ambiguous, defaults to test_failure since the post-run checks gate is
236	// test-oriented by convention.
237	func classifyGateFailure(gateResults []GateCheckResult) string {
238		for _, gr := range gateResults {
239			if gr.Status != "fail" {
240				continue
241			}
242			combined := strings.ToLower(gr.Stdout + "\n" + gr.Stderr)
243			if containsAny(combined,
244				"build failed",
245				"compilation failed",
246				"compile error",
247				"compilation error",
248				"cannot find package",
249				"undefined:",
250				"undefined reference",
251				"syntax error") {
252				return FailureModeBuildFailure
253			}
254		}
255		return FailureModeTestFailure
256	}
257	
258	// Worker-level outcome constants — set by ExecuteBead() on the result.

4		"context"
5		"fmt"

10	
11	// NoChangesContract — TD-031 §8.1. The try package owns parsing and
12	// verification for no_changes attempts and returns a structured outcome to
13	// the execute loop. The loop only applies the decision it receives.
14	type NoChangesRationaleKind string
15	
16	const (
17		// NoChangesKindVerified means the rationale included a verification_command.
18		NoChangesKindVerified NoChangesRationaleKind = "verified"
19		// NoChangesKindNeedsInvestigation means the agent asked for operator triage.
20		NoChangesKindNeedsInvestigation NoChangesRationaleKind = "needs_investigation"
21		// NoChangesKindUnjustified means neither structured marker was present.
22		NoChangesKindUnjustified NoChangesRationaleKind = "unjustified"
23	)
24	
25	const (
26		NoChangesEventVerified           = "no_changes_verified"
27		NoChangesEventUnverified         = "no_changes_unverified"
28		NoChangesEventUnjustified        = "no_changes_unjustified"
29		NoChangesEventNeedsInvestigation = "no_changes_needs_investigation"
30	)
31	
32	const (
33		NoChangesLabelUnverified         = "triage:no-changes-unverified"
34		NoChangesLabelUnjustified        = "triage:no-changes-unjustified"
35		NoChangesLabelNeedsInvestigation = "triage:needs-investigation"
36	)
37	
38	type ParsedNoChangesRationale struct {
39		Kind                     NoChangesRationaleKind
40		VerificationCommand      string
41		NeedsInvestigationReason string
42	}
43	
44	func ParseNoChangesRationale(text string) ParsedNoChangesRationale {
45		p := ParsedNoChangesRationale{Kind: NoChangesKindUnjustified}
46		needsInv := false
47		var reasonLines []string
48		inReason := false
49		for _, raw := range strings.Split(text, "\n") {
50			line := strings.TrimSpace(raw)
51			lower := strings.ToLower(line)
52			switch {
53			case strings.HasPrefix(lower, "verification_command:"):
54				cmd := strings.TrimSpace(line[len("verification_command:"):])
55				if cmd != "" {
56					p.VerificationCommand = cmd
57					p.Kind = NoChangesKindVerified
58				}
59				inReason = false
60			case strings.HasPrefix(lower, "status:"):
61				v := strings.TrimSpace(strings.ToLower(line[len("status:"):]))
62				if v == "needs_investigation" {
63					needsInv = true
64				}
65				inReason = false
66			case strings.HasPrefix(lower, "reason:"):
67				r := strings.TrimSpace(line[len("reason:"):])
68				if r != "" {
69					reasonLines = append(reasonLines, r)
70				}
71				inReason = true
72			default:
73				if inReason && line != "" {
74					reasonLines = append(reasonLines, line)
75				}
76			}
77		}
78		if p.Kind == NoChangesKindVerified {
79			return p
80		}
81		if needsInv {
82			p.Kind = NoChangesKindNeedsInvestigation
83			p.NeedsInvestigationReason = strings.Join(reasonLines, " ")
84		}
85		return p
86	}
87	
88	type VerificationCommandRunner func(ctx context.Context, projectRoot, command string) (exitCode int, output string, err error)
89	
90	const DefaultVerificationCommandTimeout = 60 * time.Second
91	
92	func DefaultVerificationCommandRunner(ctx context.Context, projectRoot, command string) (int, string, error) {
93		cctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, DefaultVerificationCommandTimeout)
94		defer cancel()
95		cmd := exec.CommandContext(cctx, "/bin/sh", "-c", command)
96		if projectRoot != "" {
97			cmd.Dir = projectRoot
98		}
99		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
100		if cctx.Err() == context.DeadlineExceeded {
101			return -1, truncateVerifyOutput(string(out)), fmt.Errorf("verification_command timed out after %s", DefaultVerificationCommandTimeout)
102		}
103		if exitErr, ok := err.(*exec.ExitError); ok {
104			return exitErr.ExitCode(), truncateVerifyOutput(string(out)), nil
105		}
106		if err != nil {
107			return -1, truncateVerifyOutput(string(out)), err
108		}
109		return 0, truncateVerifyOutput(string(out)), nil
110	}
111	
112	func truncateVerifyOutput(s string) string {
113		const max = 4 * 1024
114		if len(s) <= max {
115			return s
116		}
117		return s[:max] + "\n...[truncated]"
118	}
119	
120	type SatisfactionChecker interface {
121		CheckSatisfied(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (bool, string, error)
122	}
123	
124	type NoChangesOutcome struct {
125		Satisfied        bool
126		Action           NoChangesLifecycleAction
127		CooldownEligible bool
128		Evidence         string
129		EventKind        string
130		EventBody        string
131		Label            string
132	}
133	
134	type NoChangesLifecycleAction string
135	
136	const (
137		NoChangesActionCloseAlreadySatisfied NoChangesLifecycleAction = "close_already_satisfied"
138		NoChangesActionNeedsHumanNoCooldown  NoChangesLifecycleAction = "needs_human_no_cooldown"
139		NoChangesActionBadAttemptNoCooldown  NoChangesLifecycleAction = "bad_attempt_no_cooldown"
140		NoChangesActionRetryLaterCooldown    NoChangesLifecycleAction = "retry_later_cooldown"
141	)
142	
143	func adjudicateNoChangesContract(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report Report, projectRoot string, noChangesCount int, checker SatisfactionChecker, runner VerificationCommandRunner) (NoChangesOutcome, Report, error) {
144		if checker != nil {
145			satisfied, evidence, err := checker.CheckSatisfied(ctx, beadID, noChangesCount)
146			if err != nil {
147				return NoChangesOutcome{}, report, err
148			}
149			if satisfied {
150				if evidence != "" {
151					report.Detail = evidence
152				}
153				report.Status = StatusAlreadySatisfied
154				return NoChangesOutcome{
155					Satisfied:        true,
156					Action:           NoChangesActionCloseAlreadySatisfied,
157					CooldownEligible: false,
158					Evidence:         evidence,
159				}, report, nil
160			}
161			return NoChangesOutcome{
162				Satisfied:        false,
163				Action:           NoChangesActionBadAttemptNoCooldown,
164				CooldownEligible: false,
165			}, report, nil
166		}
167	
168		parsed := ParseNoChangesRationale(report.NoChangesRationale)
169		switch parsed.Kind {
170		case NoChangesKindVerified:
171			if runner == nil {
172				runner = DefaultVerificationCommandRunner
173			}
174			exitCode, output, runErr := runner(ctx, projectRoot, parsed.VerificationCommand)
175			body := fmt.Sprintf("verification_command=%s\nexit_code=%d", parsed.VerificationCommand, exitCode)
176			if output != "" {
177				body += "\noutput:\n" + output
178			}
179			if runErr != nil {
180				body += "\nrun_error: " + runErr.Error()
181			}
182			if runErr == nil && exitCode == 0 {
183				evidence := fmt.Sprintf("verification_command passed: %s", parsed.VerificationCommand)
184				report.Detail = evidence
185				report.Status = StatusAlreadySatisfied
186				return NoChangesOutcome{
187					Satisfied:        true,
188					Action:           NoChangesActionCloseAlreadySatisfied,
189					CooldownEligible: false,
190					Evidence:         evidence,
191					EventKind:        NoChangesEventVerified,
192					EventBody:        body,
193				}, report, nil
194			}
195			return NoChangesOutcome{
196				Satisfied:        false,
197				Action:           NoChangesActionBadAttemptNoCooldown,
198				CooldownEligible: false,
199				EventKind:        NoChangesEventUnverified,
200				EventBody:        body,
201				Label:            NoChangesLabelUnverified,
202			}, report, nil
203		case NoChangesKindNeedsInvestigation:
204			body := parsed.NeedsInvestigationReason
205			if body == "" {
206				body = "(no reason provided)"
207			}
208			return NoChangesOutcome{
209				Satisfied:        false,
210				Action:           NoChangesActionNeedsHumanNoCooldown,
211				CooldownEligible: false,
212				EventKind:        NoChangesEventNeedsInvestigation,
213				EventBody:        body,
214				Label:            NoChangesLabelNeedsInvestigation,
215			}, report, nil
216		default:
217			body := strings.TrimSpace(report.NoChangesRationale)
218			if body == "" {
219				body = "(rationale absent)"
220			}
221			return NoChangesOutcome{
222				Satisfied:        false,
223				Action:           NoChangesActionBadAttemptNoCooldown,
224				CooldownEligible: false,
225				EventKind:        NoChangesEventUnjustified,
226				EventBody:        body,
227				Label:            NoChangesLabelUnjustified,
228			}, report, nil
229		}
230	}

17	// TestNoChangesWithoutRationaleDoesNotUseNoProgressCooldown verifies TD-031's
18	// no_changes rule: absent rationale is a bad attempt / triage signal, not a
19	// retry-later condition. The configured no_progress_cooldown still resolves,
20	// but no_changes does not consume it unless the try layer returns an explicit
21	// retry-later lifecycle action.
22	func TestNoChangesWithoutRationaleDoesNotUseNoProgressCooldown(t *testing.T) {
23		const (
24			cooldown = 47 * time.Minute
25			beadID   = "ddx-npc-001"
26			fixedRev = "feedface00112233"
27		)
28	
29		projectRoot := t.TempDir()
30		ddxDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")
31		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
32	
33		// Real on-disk .ddx/config.yaml. max_no_changes_before_close is set
34		// high enough that adjudication never closes the bead — the test proves
35		// the cooldown knob is not used for unjustified no_changes.
36		cfgYAML := `version: "1.0"
37	library:
38	  path: ./library
39	  repository:
40	    url: https://github.com/example/repo
41	    branch: main
42	workers:
43	  no_progress_cooldown: "47m"
44	  max_no_changes_before_close: 99
45	`
46		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(cfgYAML), 0o644))
47	
48		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
49		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
50		require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{ID: beadID, Title: "no-progress cooldown e2e", Priority: 0}))
51	
52		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
53			Store: store,
54			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, id string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
55				return ExecuteBeadReport{
56					BeadID:    id,
57					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges,
58					SessionID: "sess-npc",
59					BaseRev:   fixedRev,
60					ResultRev: fixedRev,
61				}, nil
62			}),
63		}
64	
65		rcfg, err := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, config.CLIOverrides{Assignee: "npc-worker"})
66		require.NoError(t, err)
67		require.Equal(t, cooldown, rcfg.NoProgressCooldown(),
68			"LoadAndResolve must surface workers.no_progress_cooldown from .ddx/config.yaml")
69	
70		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
71			Once:        true,
72			ProjectRoot: t.TempDir(),
73		})
74		require.NoError(t, err)
75		require.NotNil(t, result)
76		require.Equal(t, 1, result.Attempts)
77		require.Len(t, result.Results, 1)
78	
79		retryStr := result.Results[0].RetryAfter
80		require.Empty(t, retryStr,
81			"loop must not record RetryAfter on unjustified no_changes by default")

86		_, hasRetry := got.Extra["execute-loop-retry-after"]
87		assert.False(t, hasRetry, "store must not persist execute-loop-retry-after for unjustified no_changes")

93	// TestMaxNoChangesBeforeCloseFromConfig was originally the SD-024 Stage 1
94	// behavioral proof that workers.max_no_changes_before_close in
95	// .ddx/config.yaml drove bead closure after the configured number of
96	// consecutive no_changes attempts. Under NoChangesContract (TD-031 §8.1,
97	// ddx-b24e9630) that count-based path no longer exists; the bead now closes
98	// on a verification_command marker in the rationale. The test is preserved
99	// as proof that LoadAndResolve still surfaces the knob and that a verified
100	// rationale closes on the first attempt.
101	func TestMaxNoChangesBeforeCloseFromConfig(t *testing.T) {
102		const (
103			threshold = 2
104			beadID    = "ddx-mnc-001"
105		)
106	
107		projectRoot := t.TempDir()
108		ddxDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")
109		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
110	
111		cfgYAML := `version: "1.0"
112	library:
113	  path: ./library
114	  repository:
115	    url: https://github.com/example/repo
116	    branch: main
117	workers:
118	  max_no_changes_before_close: 2
119	  no_progress_cooldown: "10m"
120	`
121		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(cfgYAML), 0o644))
122	
123		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
124		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
125		require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{ID: beadID, Title: "max no_changes before close e2e", Priority: 0}))
126	
127		var execCalls atomic.Int32
128		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
129			Store: store,
130			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, id string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
131				execCalls.Add(1)
132				return ExecuteBeadReport{
133					BeadID:             id,
134					Status:             ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges,
135					SessionID:          "sess-mnc",
136					NoChangesRationale: "verification_command: true\noutput: ok",
137				}, nil
138			}),
139			VerificationRunner: func(ctx context.Context, projectRoot, command string) (int, string, error) {
140				return 0, "ok", nil
141			},
142		}
143	
144		rcfg, err := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, config.CLIOverrides{Assignee: "mnc-worker"})
145		require.NoError(t, err)
146		require.Equal(t, threshold, rcfg.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose(),
147			"LoadAndResolve must surface workers.max_no_changes_before_close from .ddx/config.yaml")
148	
149		runtime := ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
150			Once:        true,
151			ProjectRoot: t.TempDir(),
152		}
153	
154		res1, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, runtime)
155		require.NoError(t, err)
156		require.Equal(t, 1, res1.Attempts, "iteration 1 must run exactly once")
157		require.Equal(t, 1, res1.Successes, "verified rationale closes on the first attempt")
158	
159		final, err := store.Get(beadID)
160		require.NoError(t, err)
161		assert.Equal(t, "closed", final.Status, "bead must be closed under NoChangesContract verified path")
162	
163		events, err := store.Events(beadID)
164		require.NoError(t, err)
165		var sawAlreadySatisfied bool
166		for _, ev := range events {
167			if ev.Kind == "execute-bead" {
168				if ev.Summary == ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied ||
169					ev.Body == ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied ||
170					containsToken(ev.Body, ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied) {
171					sawAlreadySatisfied = true
172				}
173			}
174		}
175		assert.True(t, sawAlreadySatisfied,
176			"expected an execute-bead event recording already_satisfied; events=%+v", events)
177	
178		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), execCalls.Load(),
179			"verified rationale closes on the first attempt; executor invoked exactly once")
180	}

182	func containsToken(body, token string) bool {
183		for i := 0; i+len(token) <= len(body); i++ {
184			if body[i:i+len(token)] == token {
185				return true
186			}
187		}
188		return false
189	}

Title:    try: count no-progress only for valid implementation attempts

Desc:     PROBLEM
The drain loop still treats no-progress mostly as base_rev == result_rev after a failed implementation attempt. That is too broad now that ddx work has pre-claim intake, pre-dispatch lint, routing preflight, review malfunctions, operator parks, and other non-implementation outcomes. Those states should not consume no-progress retry budget or trigger long cooldowns.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1964-1978 implements shouldSuppressNoProgress with only three checks: disrupted, transient outcome reason, and base/result equality.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1980-1987 only treats transport, quota, routing, timeout, merge_conflict, lock contention, and no viable provider as transient; it omits intake blocks, decomposition, claim races, routing preflight, auth/tool setup, review errors, and needs_human/operator states.
- cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:230-249 increments no_changes metadata before the work layer has a reusable valid-implementation-attempt classifier.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1199-1215 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1262-1275 apply no-progress cooldowns from separate branches instead of a shared eligibility decision.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a reusable no-progress eligibility helper, either in cli/internal/agent/try or cli/internal/agent/work, that answers whether the implementer had a real opportunity to inspect/modify/verify code.
- Count no-progress only for valid implementation attempts with checkout/context established and an implementer result available.
- Exclude intake rewrites/blocks/decomposition, claim races, routing preflight, quota, transport, auth/tool setup, review errors, needs_human/operator-required states, and other system failures.
- Route eligible no-progress stops through the StopCondition NoProgress vocabulary from ddx-89ab3fda.
- Preserve existing behavior for genuine no-commit implementation attempts.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change review verdict taxonomy beyond marking review/system outcomes no-progress-ineligible.
- Do not schedule repair cycles.
- Do not alter max-cost handling.
